breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters Claims 350GB Data Breach at European Commission
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission confirmed a significant cyberattack on its cloud infrastructure hosting the Europa.eu websites, with the hacktivist group ShinyHunters claiming responsibility for the breach. The group alleges the theft of over 350GB of data, including mail servers, databases, and confidential internal communications, which they have added to their Tor data leak site.

This incident impacts the EU's executive body and potentially affects Union entities notified of the breach, raising concerns about data sovereignty and cloud security. While the Commission stated the incident was contained without disrupting website availability, the potential exposure of sensitive government data represents a major security challenge for European institutions.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack Compromises Trivy and LiteLLM
breaking_news.summary:
The threat actor TeamPCP executed a sophisticated supply chain attack by compromising the PyPI repository, pushing malicious versions of popular packages including Trivy and LiteLLM. These compromised packages contained hidden payloads designed to steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, and CI/CD secrets from developer environments and Kubernetes clusters.

The breach has impacted over 1,000 SaaS environments and downstream victims, with Lapsus$ reportedly joining the extortion wave targeting affected organizations. This attack highlights the critical vulnerability of open-source software supply chains, as widely used AI and security tools became vectors for credential theft and persistent access.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds Critical F5 BIG-IP APM RCE to KEV Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
CISA added a critical vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (APM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after confirming active exploitation in the wild. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-53521 with a CVSS score of 9.8, allows attackers to trigger Remote Code Execution when specific access policies are enabled on virtual servers.

This vulnerability affects a widely deployed network security product used by enterprises and critical infrastructure providers globally, necessitating immediate patching to prevent system compromise. The reclassification from a Denial-of-Service issue to a critical RCE flaw underscores the evolving threat landscape and the urgency for organizations to update their F5 appliances.

breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Hackers Breach FBI Director's Personal Email
breaking_news.summary:
An Iran-linked hacking group known as Handala Hack Team successfully breached the personal Gmail account of FBI Director Kash Patel, leaking photos and documents to the internet. The group claimed credit on their website, stating that the FBI Director would now find his name among their list of successfully hacked victims.

While the FBI confirmed the incident and stated that no government or classified information was exposed, the breach of a high-profile law enforcement official's personal account is a significant reputational and security concern. This event highlights the targeting of government leadership by state-sponsored actors and the risks associated with personal digital hygiene in high-stakes roles.

breaking_news.title: CISA Warns of Critical PTC Windchill RCE Vulnerability
breaking_news.summary:
CISA and the German BSI issued urgent warnings regarding a critical Remote Code Execution vulnerability in PTC Windchill and FlexPLM software, tracked as CVE-2026-4681 with a CVSS score of 10.0. German police physically warned organizations about the flaw, which allows attackers to execute arbitrary code through the deserialization of untrusted data without authentication.

This vulnerability impacts product lifecycle management software used by major manufacturing and engineering firms, posing a severe risk to industrial operations and intellectual property. With no patch currently available, organizations are urged to implement mitigations immediately to prevent imminent exploitation by threat actors targeting critical industrial infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: China-Linked Red Menshen APT Deploys Implants in Telecom Networks
breaking_news.summary:
Rapid7 Labs uncovered a long-term espionage campaign by the China-linked APT group Red Menshen, which has infiltrated telecom networks in the Middle East and Asia. The group deploys stealthy BPFDoor kernel implants within critical infrastructure to maintain hidden access and monitor government communications over extended periods.

This campaign threatens entire populations by compromising the backbone of digital communications, allowing attackers to spy on sensitive data and potentially manipulate network traffic. The use of digital sleeper cells embedded deep within telecom environments demonstrates the sophistication of state-sponsored actors targeting critical national infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: CISA Flags Critical Langflow RCE Vulnerability Under Active Exploitation
breaking_news.summary:
CISA added a critical vulnerability in the Langflow AI framework to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after observing active exploitation within hours of disclosure. The flaw, CVE-2026-33017, allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary Python code on vulnerable instances, leading to full system compromise and data exfiltration.

As Langflow is a popular tool for building agentic AI workflows, this vulnerability exposes organizations using AI pipelines to significant risk, including the theft of environment secrets and conversation history. The rapid weaponization of the flaw highlights the need for immediate remediation in AI development environments to prevent unauthorized access and data leakage.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Linked to Operation Triangulation Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Security researchers identified the Coruna iOS exploit kit, which targets iPhones running iOS versions 13.0 through 17.2.1 using a sophisticated chain of exploits including updated code from the 2023 Operation Triangulation campaign. The kit includes five full exploit chains and 23 exploits, capable of bypassing security controls to install surveillance payloads on targeted devices.

This development suggests an evolution of state-sponsored mobile espionage capabilities, with potential links to previous high-profile campaigns targeting journalists and activists. Apple users on older iOS versions are at risk, prompting urgent updates to mitigate the threat of zero-click or watering-hole attacks leveraging these advanced exploit techniques.
